focus_spells.name: Inspire Courage
focus_spells.description: You inspire your allies with words or tunes of encouragement. You and all allies in the area gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saves against fear effects.

focus_spells.name: Counter Performance
focus_spells.trigger: You or an ally within 60 feet would be affected by an auditory or visual effect.
focus_spells.description: Your performance protects you and your allies. Roll a Performance check for a type you know—it must be an auditory performance if the trigger was auditory or a visual one for a visual trigger, and this action gains the traits for the type of performance (page 243). You and allies in the area can use the better result between your Performance check and the saving throw.

focus_spells.name: Hymn of Healing
focus_spells.description: Your glorious singing mends wounds and provides a temporary respite from harm. The target gains fast healing 2. When you Cast the Spell and the first time each round you Sustain the Spell, the target gains 2 temporary Hit Points, which last for 1 round.

heightened.+1: The fast healing and temporary Hit Points each increase by 2.

focus_spells.name: Lingering Composition
focus_spells.description: You add a flourish to your composition to extend its benefits. If your next action is to cast a cantrip composition with a duration of 1 round, attempt a Performance check. The DC is standard-difficulty of a level equal to the highestt level target of your composition.

degrees.Failure: The composition lasts 1 round, but you don't expend a focus point for trying.

feats.name: Celebrity Dedication
feats.description: The more people you have looking at you, the more content you are, and you take every opportunity to show up others when their performances are less than perfect. You gain the Upstage reaction. In addition, when you Earn Income, if the level of the task is higher than your level, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your check to Earn Income.

reactions.name: Upstage
reactions.trigger: An enemy attempts a skill check and doesn’t get a critical success
reactions.description: After your foe has tried their best, you show everyone how it’s really done. Attempt a check using the same skill that triggered this reaction. The DC is typically the same as the check the enemy attempted, though the GM can adjust this as usual.

degrees.Critical Success: You gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks until the end of your next turn.
degrees.Success: As critical success, except you gain the benefits only if the triggering creature failed their skill check.

feats.name: Bardic Lore
feats.description: Your studies cover a wide range of topics. You are trained in Bardic Lore, a special Lore skill that can be used for any checks to Recall Knowledge. If you have legendary proficiency in Occultism, you gain expert proficiency in Bardic Lore, but you can't increase your proficiency rank in Bardic Lore by any other means.

feats.name: Seedpod
feats.description: Your body produces a nearly endless supply of hard seedpods. You gain a seedpod ranged unarmed attack that deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage; these Strikes have the manipulate trait. On a critical hit, a seedpod bursts, issuing forth a tangle of rapid-growing vines that impose a -10-foot circumstance penalty to the target's Speed for 1 round.

strikes.name: Seedpod
strikes.description: Chuck one of your seed pods!

feats.name: Eyes of the City
feats.description: You can track down targets with the help of locals. You can use Diplomacy or Society, whichever you're trained in, to Track creatures in settlements. You chat with locals to help follow the trail of creatures you Track. At the GM's discretion, there might not be enough people to speak with to keep following the trail.

feats.name: Quick Identification
feats.description: You can Identify Magic swiftly. You take only 1 minute when using Identify Magic to determine the properties of an item, ongoing effect, or location, rather than 10 minutes. If you’re a master, it takes a 3-action activity, and if you’re legendary, it takes 1 action.

feats.name: Disrupting Performance
feats.description: You make an awkward or bizarre sound, note, or gesture to foul the action the creature is attempting. Your reaction gains your choice of the auditory or visual trait. Make a Performance check against the triggering creature's Will DC. Creatures with less than a +0 Intelligence gain a +4 circumstance bonus to their Will DC. Regardless of the outcome, the creature then becomes immune to this reaction for 1 minute.

reactions.name: Disrupting Performance
reactions.trigger: A creature you can observe within 30 feet takes a skill action with the Auditory, Concentrate, or Visual traits.
reactions.description: You make an awkward or bizarre sound, note, or gesture to foul the action the creature is attempting. Your reaction gains your choice of the auditory or visual trait. Make a Performance check against the triggering creature's Will DC. Creatures with less than a +0 Intelligence gain a +4 circumstance bonus to their Will DC. Regardless of the outcome, the creature then becomes immune to this reaction for 1 minute.

feats.name: Multifarious Muse
feats.description: Your muse doesn't fall into a single label. Your muse is now also a Maestro, you gain that muse's unique ability, and can now choose feats that are available only to Maestro Bards.

feats.name: Eld Attunement
feats.description: The Ostilli bound to you has learned from absorbing the primal magic you naturally exude. When you make your daily preparations, you can attune your Ostilli to a single source of potent magic to which you have been exposed for at least half of the past 24 hours, granting you a new kind of magic called an Eld Power. You may use each one only once per hour as you replenish the Ostilli's store of magical energy.

feats.name: Harmless Bystander
feats.description: You can pretend you have nothing to do with the conflict that is about to take place.

freeActions.name: Harmless Bystander
freeActions.description: Roll initiative using your Deception rather than Perception. Any non-ally creature must attempt to Sense Motive against you to realize you are hostile to them and they cannot make reactions against you until you take a hostile action against the creature or its allies. If you are wielding a weapon or cannot reasonably hide yourself as a bystander, the creature gets a +4 circumstance bonus to their Sense Motive. If you are observed taking a hostile action you are automatically recognized as hostile. Regardless of the result of the creature's Sense Motive, once you are observed being hostile, non-allied creatures are temporarily immune to your use of Harmless Bystander for 1 day.

spells.name: Daze
spells.description: You cloud the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage; the target must attempt a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also stunned 1.

spells.name: Forbidding Ward
spells.description: You ward an ally against the attacks and hostile spells from the target enemy. The target ally gains a +1 status bonus to Armor Class and saving throws against the target enemy's attacks, spells, and other effects.

spells.name: Guidance
spells.duration: Until the start of your next turn
spells.description: You ask for the guidance of supernatural entities, granting the target a +1 status bonus to one attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check the target attempts before the duration ends. The target chooses which roll to use the bonus on before rolling. If the target uses the bonus, the spell ends. Either way, the target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

spells.name: Shield
spells.duration: Until the start of your next turn
spells.description: You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use. While the spell is in effect, you can use the Shield Block reaction with your magical shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

spells.name: Haunting Hymn
spells.description: You echo a jarring hymn that only creatures in the area can hear. The hymn deals 1d8 sonic damage, with a basic Fortitude save. If a target critically fails the save, it's also deafened for 1 minute.

spells.name: Summon Undead
spells.description: You summon a creature that has the undead trait and whose level is –1 to fight for you.

heightened.2nd: The creature can be up to Level 1.
heightened.3rd: The creature can be up to Level 2.
heightened.4th: The creature can be up to Level 3.
heightened.5th: The creature can be up to Level 5.
heightened.6th: The creature can be up to Level 7.
heightened.7th: The creature can be up to Level 9.
heightened.8th: The creature can be up to Level 11.
heightened.9th: The creature can be up to Level 13.
heightened.10th: The creature can be up to Level 15.

spells.name: Bless
spells.description: Blessings from beyond help your companions strike true. You and your allies gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls while within the emanation. Once per round on subsequent turns, you can Sustain the spell to increase the emanation's radius by 10 feet. Bless can counteract bane.

spells.name: Fear
spells.description: You plant fear in the target; it must attempt a Will save.

heightened.3rd: You can target up to five creatures.

degrees.Critical Failure: The target is frightened 3 and fleeing for 1 round.

spells.name: Charitable Urge
spells.description: You speak on the virtue of charity, compelling the target to give away its possessions. The target must attempt a Will save. If the target has no items on its person, the spell fails.

degrees.Success: The target is stunned 1 as it wrestles with the urge.
degrees.Failure: On its next turn, before it does anything else, the target must present the nearest creature with an item in its possession; the target chooses which item to give, and if the only item it has is one that it's currently using to defend itself, such as a weapon during a combat encounter, it can choose to be stunned for 1 round instead of giving up the item. This might require the target to Interact to retrieve an item or move to reach the nearest creature, and passing the item off requires an Interact action as normal.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except the duration is 4 rounds, and the target must repeat the effects of a failure on each of its turns. At the end of each of its turns, the target can attempt a new Will save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round, ending the effects entirely on a critical success.

spells.name: Noise Blast
spells.description: A cacophonous noise blasts out, dealing 2d10 sonic damage. Each creature must attempt a Fortitude save.

degrees.Failure: The target takes full damage and is Deafened for 1 round
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature takes double damage and is deafened for 1 minute, and stunned 1.

powers.name: Erode
powers.description: You touch a creature, ushering them towards the harsh embrace of death. Make a melee spell attack roll against a foe in reach, dealing 2d10 (+1d10 per level) void damage.

degrees.Critical Success: The target succumbs to the call of the Void and takes double damage
degrees.Failure: The target takes damage equal to 2 + half your level
degrees.Critical Failure: The target resists, taking no damage

powers.name: Rust Cloud
powers.description: Rust pours out of your mouth and eyes, creating a cloud of rust that extends 20-feet in every direction. All creatures inside the cloud are Concealed, and creatures outside the cloud are concealed to them. You continue producing this cloud until you spend 2 actions to Dismiss the effect, 10 minutes pass, or you fall unconscious, whichever comes first.

special.name: Shrouded
special.description: When a creature is shrouded, all light within 20 feet of them is lowered in intensity by one step (bright to dim, dim to dark).

powers.name: Gloaming Backstab
powers.description: You reach into the shadows and pull out a blade of pure darkness. You deal 2d6 (+1d6 per level) void damage to a foe within your reach with a Melee Spell Attack. If you're hidden, you deal an additional 2d4 (+1d4 per level) percision damage. You become Shrouded until the end of your turn.

powers.name: Chains of Rust
powers.description: Rusted chains erupt from the ground, ensnaring your foes. Choose one of your space's four sides; all foes adjacent to you along that side take 2d6 (+1d6 per level) bludgeoning damage with a basic Fortitude Save.

degrees.Failure: All movement is difficult terrain for the creature until the start of your next turn.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature is immobilized until the start of your next turn.

powers.name: Addling Blast
powers.description: You blast a cone of disorienting mental energy to muddle your foes' minds and throw them off guard. You deal 1d4 (+1d4 per level) mental damage to all creatures in a 15-foot cone with a basic Will save.

degrees.Success: The creature is Off-Guard until the start of their next turn
degrees.Failure: The creature is Off-Guard and Stupefied 1 until the end of your next turn
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature is Off-Guard and Stupefied 2 until the end of your next turn

powers.name: Psychic Thrust
powers.description: You thrust psychic energy into a foe from a distance to overwhelm their mind. Make a ranged Spell Attack against a foe in range, dealing 2d6 (+1d6 per level) mental damage

degrees.Critical Success: You hit a mental weak spot, dealing double damage and inflicting Stupefied 3 until the end of your next turn
degrees.Success: The enemy takes regular damage and is Stupefied 2 until the end of your next turn
degrees.Failure: The enemy takes no damage, and is Stupefied 1 until the end of your next turn
degrees.Critical Failure: The enemy's psyche is too strong and they are unaffected

special.name: Limned
special.description: A limned creature is surrounded by light, causing it to glow like a torch, outlining it and making its position obvious. A limned creature cant normally become concealed, though if the creature would become invisible it will become concealed instead. Attempts at stealth while Limned take a -4 penalty.

powers.name: Stupefying Inception
powers.description: You touch a foe and release overflowing mental energy into their vulnerable mind, damaging them and briefly weakening their psychic defenses. Make a Spell Attack against an enemy; you deal 2d8 (+1d8 per level) mental damage to them.

degrees.Critical Success: You deal double damage, and they are Stupefied 3 until the end of your next turn
degrees.Success: You deal regular damage, and they are Stupefied 2 until the end of your next turn
degrees.Failure: They take no damage, but are Stupefied 1 until the end of your next turn
degrees.Critical Failure: They resist your psychic assault and take no damage

powers.name: Shining Guidance
powers.description: You call forth a guiding light to surround your foe and guide your attacks toward them. Choose an enemy in range; you deal 2d4 (+1d4 per level + half your level) fire damage with a Basic Fortitude Save.

degrees.Critical Success: The creature resists entirely and is unaffected
degrees.Success: The enemy is Limned until the end of your next turn. You can choose to deal no fire damage rather than half; if you do, you and your allies get a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against them until the end of your next turn, as long as it remains limned
degrees.Failure: The foe is Limned until the end of your next turn. You and your allies get a +1 circumstance bonus on attack rolls against the creature until the end of your next turn, as long as it remains limned
degrees.Critical Failure: The foe is limned for two rounds (ending at the end of your turn). During that time, as long as it remains limned, you and your allies gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against them

special.name: Charged
special.description: Using electric Eld abilities gives you Charge; while charged, you are off-guard against attacks that deal electricity damage and take a -2 on saves against electric effects. The charged condition lasts until the encounter ends, you take electric damage, or you Discharge to empower another Electric Eld Power.

powers.name: Electric Surge
powers.description: You send a surging current of electricity in front of you, shocking creatures in a line. You deal 1d4 (+1d4 per level) electric damage to all creatures in a 20ft line with a Basic Reflex Save. Choose one rider effect: you either become Charged, or you Discharge to increase the are to a 40ft line and the damage increases from d4s to d8s.

powers.name: Polarize
powers.description: You electrically polarize the target, causing it to become charged as electricity surges through them, jolting them over and over. You deal 1d4 persistent electricity damage per two levels you have to a target within 30 ftt with a Basic Fortitude Save. On a failure they become Charged for 2 rounds. The persistent electricity damage from Polarize doesn't discharge their Charged condition. Choose one rider effect: you either become Charged, or you Discharge to increase the persistent electricity damage to 1d4 per level you have.
